title: make --pod conflict with --hostname in podman run

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **NOT APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *chenzhiwei* To complete the [pull request process](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process), please assign **giuseppe** You can assign the PR to them by writing `/assign @giuseppe` in a comment when ready. The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod).
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - **[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)** Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

Hi @chenzhiwei. Thanks for your PR.

I'm waiting for a [containers](https://github.com/orgs/containers/people) or [openshift](https://github.com/orgs/openshift/people) member to verify that this patch is reasonable to test. If it is, they should reply with `/ok-to-test` on its own line. Until that is done, I will not automatically test new commits in this PR, but the usual testing commands by org members will still work. Regular contributors should [join the org](https://github.com/orgs/openshift/people) to skip this step.

Once the patch is verified, the new status will be reflected by the `ok-to-test` label.

I understand the commands that are listed [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod).

<details>

Instructions for interacting with me using PR comments are available [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/pull-requests.md).  If you have questions or suggestions related to my behavior, please file an issue against the [kubernetes/test-infra](https://github.com/kubernetes/test-infra/issues/new?title=Prow%20issue:) repository.
</details>

Not sure if this is the right approach or not and I also find that `--pod` also conflicts with `--network` or maybe other flags.

@rhatdan @mheon @haircommander
I am fine with this, but I think @haircommander will point out that you could have told the pod to not share uts namespace, which would then allow both options at the same time.
If we make this change or similar, we should have the restrictions noted in the man pages somewhere.

Disagree that this is how we should handle things. During spec generation, after we've figured out what namespaces the pod shares, is a much better fit - detect that we're sharing the UTS namespace and then note it conflicts with `--hostname`
Hi @haircommander, my concern is there is no way to export the pod yaml file with containers have different hostname. Kubernetes pod does not support different hostname of containers inside a pod.
@chenzhiwei I would say that means we shouldn't show the hostname in the pod yaml if the pod doesn't share a uts namespace
@chenzhiwei most folks aren't going to change the namespaces a pod shares, but I want to remain consistent with the design decision that sharing/not sharing namespaces is allowed

When creating pod with play kube and users specify the `hostname` in the yaml means that users want to share UTS namespace.

When creating pod with pod create, users can do whatever they want.

Seems we don't need this PR anymore, the current logic is fine.

Should I close this PR as well as this feature request https://github.com/containers/libpod/issues/3840?
If this is already covered for the CLI case, I think we're good to close.
